text: All debate schedules and city election notices in San Isidro City are posted on the official election bulletin and city website.
Candidate A, Aurelia Santos, is the incumbent mayor and has completed one full term of 4 years.
Candidate B, Bruno Villanueva, announced his mayoral bid 6 months ago and has no prior citywide elected office experience.
Candidate C, Celia Navarro, is running on a law-and-order platform and has not released a full line-item budget plan.
Candidate A's public health expansion ordinance is already in Phase 1 rollout and can be verified in the city council archive.
Candidate B's student transit subsidy proposal still requires city council approval before implementation.
No formal endorsement record exists between Candidate A and any private construction firm in the city's public filings.
Candidate C's campaign speeches on crime often cite national statistics rather than city-specific crime records.
Candidate B's open-data proposal would publish city procurement summaries every quarter if passed.
The city's reported crime rate decreased over the last two years under Candidate A's administration according to the city report.
Attendance records show Candidate A joined 34 of 36 scheduled barangay town halls during the current term.
Candidate B has published costings for his top three proposals on the official campaign website.
Candidate C has not posted a formal procurement-transparency plan as of the latest campaign update.
